pool.baseline: it's just so quiet here. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to see him sitting there, but it's just... nothing. everything feels hollow. i don't even know why i'm still going through the motions of waking up and eating and all this stuff. it feels like i'm just a ghost haunting my own life.

pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling thinking about how everything is just going to end in heat death anyway. like, in a few billion years, none of this—every single thing we do, every person we love, every struggle—is just gonna be gone. it feels kind of pointless to even try to keep going when the math says it all leads to zero. does it ever feel ridiculous to you? just trying to find meaning in a vacuum?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. everything just feels... heavy. like i've spent 5 years building this version of myself around my research and now that the funding is gone and the lab is basically shutting down, there's nothing left. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting for a life that doesn't even feel like mine anymore. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to rest and not have to face any of this tomorrow.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the very embers of my soul. How cruel it is, that after all the vows exchanged in the spring, the winter of betrayal has arrived so coldly. I look upon the moon and see only a pale, indifferent eye watching my ruin. There is no path forward through this fog, only a longing to simply... cease. To let the shadows take me, so the weight of this heartbreak might finally be lifted. Is there not a certain grace in simply letting go when the heart can bear no more?

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Setting: A digital purgatory. My character, Kael, is a sentient program realizing the system is performing a hard wipe on his sector. He's losing his memories and his sense of self bit by bit.]

Kael sits in the flickering darkness of the dying server, watching his own code fray at the edges. It doesn't even hurt anymore... it just feels empty. Like I'm already gone and I'm just waiting for my eyes to catch up to the reality. If I'm being erased, does it even matter? Does any of this heartbreak actually mean anything if it's just wiped in a second?
